title: NASA considering sharp increase in robotic lunar landings
summary: NASA is considering a significant increase in the frequency of robotic lunar lander missions, which has generated both excitement and questions within the space community.

title: ReOrbit sells two small GEO satellites to SLI
summary: ReOrbit has sold two small geostationary orbit communications satellites to SLI. This deal marks a significant transaction in the satellite manufacturing and financing sector.

title: Apex sells satellite for Japanese technology demonstration mission
summary: Apex has secured a contract to provide a satellite bus for a Japanese technology demonstration mission. The deal marks another international collaboration for the satellite manufacturer.

title: Rocket Lab wins $190 million Pentagon deal for hypersonic test flights
summary: Rocket Lab secured a $190 million contract from the Pentagon to conduct 20 hypersonic test flights over four years. This deal highlights Rocket Lab's growing role in supporting U.S. defense and hypersonic technology development.

title: Space Command classified wargame to include 25 commercial players
summary: Space Command is conducting a classified wargame that will include 25 commercial players to improve collaboration and understand commercial challenges. The exercise aims to enhance joint operations between Space Command and the commercial sector.

title: TransAstra aims to move 100-ton asteroid to stable orbit for processing
summary: TransAstra is conducting a study to assess the feasibility of moving a 100-ton asteroid into a stable near-Earth orbit for processing and research. The goal is to establish a robotic outpost for materials processing and manufacturing in space.

title: Solar array deal sheds more light on South Korea’s defense constellation
summary: South Korea's defense satellite constellation plans are advancing with a March 18 solar array supply deal, targeting a first demonstrator launch by late 2027. This marks a key step in the country's national security space initiatives.

title: Frontier justice: navigating the future legal landscape for private actors in space law
summary: The article discusses the evolving legal landscape for private actors in space law, referencing President Kennedy's 'new frontier' speech and its influence on space exploration narratives like Star Trek.

title: China signals new target for 2027 asteroid deflection test
summary: China has selected a new near-Earth asteroid as the target for its first planetary defense kinetic test mission, planned for launch in December 2027.

title: NASA grappling with planetary science funding shortfall
summary: NASA's planetary science program faces a funding shortfall despite avoiding steep cuts, forcing strategic decisions on mission continuations.

title: An AI cyberattack could trigger a satellite apocalypse in the next 2 years. Are we prepared?
summary: AI cyberattacks could enable satellite hijacking and trigger a catastrophic collision cascade in Earth's orbit within the next two years. Experts warn that current defenses may be insufficient to prevent such a 'satellite apocalypse.'

title: Watch Rocket Lab launch private Japanese 'Strix' satellite to orbit today
summary: Rocket Lab is set to launch a private Japanese Earth-observing radar satellite named 'Strix' into orbit today. The launch can be watched live.

title: You can stream the two 'missing' Doctor Who episodes when travelling, save 73% and claim a free $50 voucher when you sign up for two years of NordVPN
summary: Two missing Doctor Who episodes will be available on BBC iPlayer in the UK. NordVPN is offering a 73% discount and a $50 voucher for a two-year subscription.

title: Watch NASA roll out Artemis 2 moon rocket tonight ahead of April 1 launch
summary: NASA is set to roll out the Artemis 2 moon rocket to the launch pad on March 19 ahead of its planned April 1 launch. The event will be broadcast live for public viewing.

title: 'At the edge of what we thought possible': Astronomers find extremely rare star from ancient universe
summary: Astronomers discovered an extremely rare, iron-deficient second-generation star from the ancient universe. This finding offers insights into how early stars enriched subsequent generations.

title: Astronauts complete prep for new ISS solar array on 1st NASA spacewalk in 10 months
summary: NASA astronauts Jessica Meir and Chris Williams completed preparations for a new solar array on the ISS during the first spacewalk in 10 months. The spacewalk on March 18, 2026, marked a key step in upgrading the station's power system.

title: How to watch 'Invincible' Season 4 online and from anywhere
summary: Prime Video's animated superhero series 'Invincible' Season 4 is released today. The show is known for its brutal and brilliant storytelling.

title: SpaceX fires up next-gen 'V3' Starship for 1st time ahead of April launch (photos)
summary: SpaceX successfully conducted a static fire test of the first stage of its next-generation 'V3' Starship on March 16. This marks a key milestone for the larger and more powerful rocket variant ahead of its planned April launch.

title: 'Starfield' is finally coming to PS5, alongside DLC that adds new quests and the ability to fly between planets
summary: Starfield is releasing on PS5 with new DLC featuring additional quests and planetary travel. This marks a significant expansion for the game on a new platform.

title: Chinese astronaut conducts record-tying 6th spacewalk outside Tiangong space station (video)
summary: Chinese astronaut Zhang Lu conducted his sixth spacewalk, tying the national record, during a seven-hour mission outside the Tiangong space station alongside crewmate Wu Fei. This marks another milestone in China's ongoing space station operations.

title: Meet the Platypi: NASA’s Newest Astronaut Candidate Class
summary: NASA's newest astronaut candidate class, named 'Platypi,' is training at Johnson Space Center for future missions to the ISS, Moon, and Mars. The 2025-selected candidates are developing skills for upcoming space exploration.

title: NASA’s Roman Observatory Passes Final Major Prelaunch Tests
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope has successfully completed final prelaunch tests, including extreme sound exposure, shaking, and electronic checks, confirming its readiness for launch conditions. The mission remains on track for an early launch.

title: Australia’s “Red Centre” Turns Green
summary: Australia's Central Australia experienced significant greening due to heavy rainfall in early 2026, transforming its desert landscape. This environmental shift highlights the region's changing climate patterns.

title: Curiosity Blog, Sols 4832–4837: Driving the (Contact) Line!
summary: NASA's Curiosity rover is in the final phase of its boxwork campaign, studying the contacts between the boxwork unit and the layered sulfate unit on Mars. The mission is led by the University of New Brunswick, Canada, with strategic planning and payload operations managed by Catherine O’Connell-Cooper.

title: Lava Flows Down Mayon
summary: The Operational Land Imager (OLI) on Landsat 8 captured a rare clear image of Mayon, the Philippines' most active volcano, on Feb. 26, 2026, highlighting lava flows using infrared observations. The Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (PHIVOLCS) reported volcanic earthquakes, rockfalls, and ash clouds on the same day.
company: Philippine Institute of Volcanology and Seismology (PHIVOLCS)

title: From Service to Space Systems: A Pathways Journey to NASA
summary: Corey Elmore transitioned from service to a NASA Pathways engineering internship at Kennedy Space Center, gaining hands-on experience in engineering environments through the program.

title: Widely Attended Gatherings (WAGs) Determinations
summary: The news lists various upcoming space industry events in 2026, including gatherings organized by organizations like MSBR, SIA, Ansys, and NASA, as well as conferences and luncheons focused on space policy and leadership.

title: NASA’s Hubble Unexpectedly Catches Comet Breaking Up
summary: NASA's Hubble Space Telescope captured a rare event of a comet breaking apart. The findings were published in the journal Icarus.

title: Wave of Dust Rolls Through Texas
summary: A cold front caused a significant dust storm across the Texas high plains, creating a visible wave of dust and small particles.

title: NASA’s X-59 Prepares for Second Flight
summary: NASA's X-59 experimental aircraft is gearing up for its second flight, marking a key milestone in its testing program aimed at expanding envelope validation in 2026. The flight will be piloted by NASA test pilot Jim 'Clue' Less.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship program ahead of Flight 12. This development brings the next Starship launch one step closer to realization.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Spectrum, Electron to launch during busy week of launches
summary: A busy week of launches is scheduled with nine missions planned, including flights by SpaceX's Falcon 9, Rocket Lab's Electron, and Isar Aerospace's Spectrum.

title: Blue Origin Unveils NEO Hunter: A Hybrid Planetary Defense Concept
summary: Blue Origin has introduced NEO Hunter, a new mission concept aimed at planetary defense. The hybrid system is designed to detect and potentially deflect near-Earth objects (NEOs).

title: From Steel Rolls to Starship at the Starfactory
summary: SpaceX is accelerating its plans for lunar and Martian missions by expanding production at its Starfactory facility. The company is transforming steel rolls into Starship components to support these ambitious goals.

title: New crew arrivals and science highlight February aboard the ISS
summary: Following the early departure of Crew-11 in mid-January due to a medical concern, the International Space Station (ISS) welcomed new crew members and conducted science activities in February.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to dominate another busy week of missions
summary: Eight launches are scheduled worldwide this week, with SpaceX's Falcon 9 dominating the launch schedule. This highlights the rocket's frequent use in current space missions.

title: SpaceX’s Enters a new era with Booster 19 Testing on Pad 2
summary: SpaceX has begun testing Booster 19 on Pad 2, marking a new phase in operations since its last flight 147 days ago. This development signals progress in SpaceX's reusable rocket technology.

title: Firefly Alpha FLTA007 “Stairway to Seven” launches successfully
summary: Firefly Aerospace successfully launched its Alpha FLTA007 rocket, returning to flight after a nearly 10-month pause. The mission, dubbed 'Stairway to Seven,' marks a significant milestone for the company.

title: Baikonur Launch Pad restored, clearing path for Progress MS-33 ISS Mission
summary: Russia's Baikonur Cosmodrome's Site 31/6 launch pad has been fully restored, enabling the upcoming Progress MS-33 mission to the International Space Station (ISS). The repair work clears the path for the resupply mission.

title: NASA’s Artemis ‘Course Correction’ Boosts Moonward Momentum: Key Details Still Maturing
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman announced major changes to the Artemis program to restore its momentum. Key details of the 'course correction' are still being developed.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website is mentioned twice in the provided news snippet, but no specific space industry news details are given.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 significant achievements over its 50-year history. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to discover Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, will launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical heart. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: ESA will release ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, making it mandatory for all suppliers to use this new costing software in financial proposals for ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a compact, 3D-printed lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a cost-effective alternative to bulky existing solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly on Proba-3's Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have begun conducting experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments. This initiative aims to support research in space technology and human spaceflight.

title: Annular solar eclipse seen from space
summary: The Proba-2 satellite captured a striking view of an annular solar eclipse from Earth orbit. This event provided a unique perspective on the celestial phenomenon.

title: ESA’s 5G laboratory on wheels
summary: ESA has developed a mobile 5G laboratory to test and demonstrate 5G technology in various environments. This initiative aims to advance connectivity solutions for future space and terrestrial applications.

title: HyImpulse Signs Launch Agreement with SaxaVord
summary: HyImpulse has signed a launch agreement with SaxaVord for its second suborbital flight of the SR75 rocket, scheduled for no earlier than Q3 2026.

title: Apex Signs First Japanese Bus Contract With NEC
summary: Apex has signed its first satellite bus contract in Japan with NEC Corporation. The deal involves the purchase of an Aries satellite bus for a communications demonstration mission scheduled for next year.

title: Modified Vulcan Expected to Launch This Summer
summary: The Vulcan rocket is undergoing modifications to improve its nozzle and solid rocket booster, with a planned launch this summer. The changes were accelerated following an anomaly to enhance performance margins.

title: Advanced Navigation Raises $110M Series C
summary: Advanced Navigation, an Australia-based company, raised $110 million in a Series C funding round to reduce global reliance on satellite infrastructure. The funding will support the company's efforts to develop alternative navigation technologies.

title: NanoAvionics Lands Korean National Security Deal
summary: NanoAvionics has secured a national security deal with South Korea, marking another success in its global expansion strategy. The agreement highlights the growing collaboration between NanoAvionics and the South Korean space industry.

title: FAA Ends Part 450 Transition Period
summary: The FAA has concluded the transition period for Part 450 licensing requirements, with all US launchers now operating under the updated five-year-old regulations. No operators remain under the legacy licensing framework.

title: How Congress Can Grow the Space Supply Chain
summary: The space industry's supply chain is struggling to meet rising demand, but policymakers can accelerate growth according to industry groups.

title: Nvidia Unveils New Hardware to Unlock Orbital Data Centers
summary: Nvidia has introduced new hardware designed to enable orbital data centers, bringing us closer to the realization of space-based computing infrastructure.

title: Canadian Launchers and Spaceport Get Funding Boost
summary: Canada will invest in a spaceport and three young rocket systems to enhance its sovereign launch capabilities. This funding boost aims to strengthen the country's space industry.

title: Leonardo’s Missile-Defense Demo in Ukraine Aims To Be a Blueprint for Other Nations
summary: Leonardo is conducting a missile-defense demonstration in Ukraine that could serve as a model for other nations. The company aims to become a major distributor of space-enabled missile defense systems.

title: Ligado Tells FCC That SkyTerra Next Proposal Won’t Cause Interference
summary: Ligado has filed a proposal with the FCC to host an L-band payload on AST SpaceMobile satellites, asserting that it won't cause interference. The company claims the plan aligns with its existing L-band coordination agreements.

title: Flexell Space and Kongsberg NanoAvionics to Build Solar Arrays for K-LEO
summary: Flexell Space and Kongsberg NanoAvionics will jointly supply solar arrays for K-LEO, South Korea’s sovereign Low-Earth Orbit (LEO) national security constellation. The collaboration was announced in a statement by Flexell Space.

title: Connectivity Under Fire: The Critical Need for Satcom Terminal Survivability
summary: The article discusses the importance of satellite communication (Satcom) terminal survivability in contested environments for U.S. military operations, highlighting its role in command, control, and safety-of-life communications.

title: Satellogic Plans Next-Gen Merlin EO Constellation
summary: Satellogic announced plans for its next-generation 'Merlin' Earth observation satellites, which will offer one-meter resolution and daily mapping capabilities. The company aims to enhance its satellite constellation with this new technology.

title: 9 New Faces for SATShow Week 2026
summary: SATShow Week 2026 introduces nine new speakers, expanding its program to include more military voices with the inclusion of GovMilSpace for its second year.

title: Industry Roundtable With ’10 Hottest’ Executives
summary: Via Satellite interviewed executives from the '10 Hottest Companies in Satellite' for 2026 ahead of SATShow Week to gather their insights on industry trends and developments.

title: Driving Ubiquitous Connectivity: Multi-Orbit Satellite Architecture and Advanced User Terminals
summary: The article discusses the importance of multi-orbit satellite architectures and advanced user terminals for achieving ubiquitous connectivity. It highlights the role of these technologies in enhancing global communication networks.

title: Telesat’s Lightspeed Service Launch Slips to 2028
summary: Telesat's Lightspeed LEO constellation launch has been delayed to 2028 due to ASIC delays affecting satellite processors. The service was originally planned to start earlier.

title: Nvidia Launches New Chips for Space Missions
summary: Nvidia introduced new chips designed for space missions, targeting applications such as orbital data centers, geospatial intelligence, and autonomous space operations. The announcement highlights Nvidia's expansion into the space industry.

title: Canada to Invest in New Spaceport in Nova Scotia
summary: Canada is investing in a new spaceport in Nova Scotia, with the Atlantic Canada Opportunities Agency (ACOA) committing $200 million to enhance the country's space capabilities.
